title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 379.017 - Combined risk policies authorized — single premium may be lower than aggregate coverage rates
text:
379.017.  Combined risk policies authorized — single premium may be lower than aggregate coverage rates. — Every insurance company licensed to do business in this state and authorized to make insurance on classes of insurance enumerated in subdivisions (1), (2) and (3) of subsection 1 of section 379.010 shall have authority to combine in single policies of insurance the perils of fire and allied lines with any one or more perils of casualty, fidelity, surety and inland marine insurance, which such company is authorized to make, and may charge therefor one indivisible premium or rate which may differ from the aggregate premium or rate applicable to separate policies covering the same property and risk or risks, and the difference in rates or premiums shall not be deemed to be unfairly discriminatory under the provisions of chapter 375 and this chapter.
(L. 1959 H.B. 249 §§ 1, 2, A.L. 1965 p. 586, A.L. 1967 p. 516, A.L. 1972 S.B. 547, A.L. 1989 S.B. 250)
